task_name: LAP Simulator - Navigation and Scope Orientation Exercise
summary: This video is instructional content demonstrating a laparoscopic simulator exercise, not an official FLS task. The exercise involves navigating a 30-degree angled scope to locate numbered boxes (1-10) on a virtual box trainer. This is a spatial orientation and scope handling drill, commonly used as preliminary training before FLS tasks.
video_type_explanation: The video contains narrated instructions, on-screen prompts ('Find the next number'), and demonstration of technique rather than a timed performance being scored. It shows the LAP (Laparoscopic Abdominal Procedural) Simulator training environment.
why_not_scored: This is not one of the five official FLS MISTELS tasks (Peg Transfer, Pattern Cut, Endoloop, Extracorporeal Suture, Intracorporeal Suture). It is a preparatory scope familiarization exercise.

demonstrated_techniques: Spatial navigation in a restricted field of view
Locating and focusing on numbered targets sequentially
verbal_cues: Find a number to start the exercise
Focus the scope on the number in order to complete the exercise
common_errors_addressed: Difficulty maintaining scope focus on small numbered markers
Inefficient searching patterns when scope angle is restricted
Loss of spatial orientation with angled optics
teaching_content.purpose: Preliminary laparoscopic scope familiarization and spatial reasoning before advancing to formal FLS tasks

cannot_determine: Official FLS score — this is not an FLS task
